title: rhaponticum scariosum subsp. heleniifolium
intro: Rhaponticum scariosum subsp. heleniifolium, also known as the Horseheal or Tengmalm's Leatherflower, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to Northern Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. Rhaponticum scariosum subsp. heleniifolium has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, improving liver function, and acting as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
